---
title: "One or more mounts listed in fstab cannot be mounted"
date: 2009-11-24
forum: General Help
---

### Post by anjanesh on 2009-11-24
All of a sudden my ubuntu 9.10 PC goes crazy.
Screen flickering wildly - coludnt do anything - once in a while half image of an application pops up - mouse stuck most times - was afraid this would be my first linux virus !

Cold rebooted.
On boot it showed **One or more mounts listed in fstab cannot be mounted**.

```
~$ cat /etc/fstab
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=7755f3c8-9002-41b0-b434-3750a6c712fb / ext3 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda3 :
UUID=ee78dd9e-dc59-44c7-b6e6-c9508691f8b0 /home ext3 relatime 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=b882e67c-3123-4396-b841-11563d976aff none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
```

I have one HDD - 3 partitions : /, /home and swap

How can diagnose this ?

---

### Post by dcstar on 2009-11-24
> **anjanesh said:**
> All of a sudden my ubuntu 9.10 PC goes crazy.
Screen flickering wildly - coludnt do anything - once in a while half image of an application pops up - mouse stuck most times - was afraid this would be my first linux virus !

Cold rebooted.
On boot it showed **One or more mounts listed in fstab cannot be mounted**.
..........

You had some sort of system crash, you rebooted without cleanly unmounting the filesystems, you probably now have filesystem corruption.

Boot off a Live CD and run fsck on the HD partitions.

---

### Post by anjanesh on 2009-11-24
> Boot off a Live CD and run fsck on the HD partitions.
1. Can I use 8.04 CD  - I dont have a 9.10 CD right now.

2. Its a 500GB HDD - 95GB / , 365GB /home, 2GB /swap and none set aside for /boot.
Is it better to reformat with 100MB set aside  for /boot and ext4 instead of ext3 ?

---

### Post by dcstar on 2009-11-25
> **anjanesh said:**
> 1. Can I use 8.04 CD  - I dont have a 9.10 CD right now.

2. Its a 500GB HDD - 95GB / , 365GB /home, 2GB /swap and none set aside for /boot.
Is it better to reformat with 100MB set aside  for /boot and ext4 instead of ext3 ?

[LIST=1]
[*]Yes.
[*]It is basically pointless having a separate /boot unless your are running some sort of RAID setup.
[/LIST]

### Post by shaibn on 2009-12-01
You should check out [this]("http://newyork.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=8151569&postcount=10") solution (worked for me).

> **kentaur said:**
> Your installs have failed.

remount your filesystem like this:

```

mount -o remount,rw / 
```then do a 

```
sudo dpkg --configure -a 
```And the installation will continue.

When you get the error, hit Enter. Type in the root password and continue solution above.

---
